title: Babylon
logline: In 1920s Hollywood, an ambitious Mexican immigrant, a wild-child starlet, and a fading silent film star navigate the industry's chaotic transition from silent pictures to talkies, as their dreams collide with the brutal realities of a changing era.
antagonistOrConflict: The transition to sound cinema and the industry's ruthless machinery that destroys those who can't adapt
tone: Epic, chaotic, bittersweet, darkly comedic, tragic
themes: The death of an era and the cost of progress
The American Dream and its corruption
The disposability of people in Hollywood
The gap between public image and private reality

actBreaks.description: Manny witnesses the premiere of The Jazz Singer in New York, experiencing the seismic shift that sound will bring to cinema and everyone's lives.

validation.ruling: Scene #76 (Warner Theatre screening of The Jazz Singer) is the irreversible turning point ending Act 1: Manny witnesses the thunderous audience reaction to synchronized sound ('You ain't heard nothin yet'), fundamentally changing the story's direction as the entire industry must now reckon with talkies. This is the last scene before the time jump to sound filmmaking, making it the proper act break.

actBreaks.description: Elinor St. John's devastating interview with Jack Conrad is published, marking the point of no return for the old guard and the complete collapse of the silent era's dreams.

validation.ruling: Scene #132 (Elinor's office confrontation) serves as Act 2's climactic break: Elinor brutally tells Jack 'Your time is up. There is no why'—an irreversible declaration of his obsolescence that forces him into the final act's descent. This fundamentally shifts the story from Jack fighting for relevance to Jack facing inevitable decline, occurring at 73.8% and functioning as the crucial turning point before the resolution.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This is a triptych structure masquerading as three-act: the silent era (chaos and possibility), the transition (struggle and failure), and the aftermath (reckoning and memory). The first movement is pure Dionysian energy—the Wallach party sequence (scenes 4-22) establishes the anything-goes ethos of 1926 Hollywood. The battlefield shoot (scenes 33-47) demonstrates how this chaos translates into art-making. Act One ends not with a traditional plot turn but with a technological apocalypse: scene 76, where Manny sees The Jazz Singer and realizes everything is about to change.

The second movement is structured around failure and adaptation. The brutal soundstage sequence (scene 93, starting page 91) is the structural and thematic center—a 10-page tour de force where we watch Nellie's inability to adapt in real-time. The dinner party disaster follows, and the act closes with Elinor's article destroying Jack. The final movement is elegiac, tracking the characters' various ends and Manny's survival as the only one who successfully transforms. The structure mirrors the industry itself: explosive creativity giving way to technical constraint giving way to corporate consolidation.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately bipolar. The opening party (scenes 4-22) sprawls across 24 pages of controlled chaos. The battlefield sequence maintains breakneck energy through rapid cross-cutting between three simultaneous shoots. Then scene 93 (the soundstage nightmare) slows to excruciating real-time, spending 10 pages on a single failed take to viscerally demonstrate how sound killed spontaneity. The script accelerates again through montage sequences (scenes 48-60, 89-96) showing success, then decelerates into the long, painful dinner party. The final act uses time jumps (cards reading specific dates) to show years passing in moments, creating a sense of inevitable decline. This isn't traditional three-act pacing—it's operatic, with movements that swell and contract based on emotional intensity rather than plot mechanics.

incitingIncident.description: The ballroom is in chaos with drunken guests, a jazz band led by trumpeter Sidney Palmer, and a brawl that Sidney calmly avoids while continuing to play.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: 7 INT. BALLROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 7

validation.ruling: Scene #4 is merely arrival at a location, not a story-shattering event. Scene #7 (the chaotic ballroom party) better serves as the inciting incident: it's where we're introduced to the decadent world of 1920s Hollywood that will be upended, establishing the status quo that sound will destroy and introducing key characters in their element before everything changes.

midpoint.description: Nellie struggles through multiple takes on a silent soundstage in oppressive heat as the director and crew demand she hit precise marks for the microphone while staying natural.

validation.ruling: Scene #93 marks the definitive shift from silent to sound filmmaking (July 1928), transforming the entire production environment from chaotic open-air sets to oppressive soundstages. This is a clear point of no return—the industry cannot revert to silent films, stakes are raised as characters must adapt or perish, and it occurs at 48.7% of the script, perfectly positioned as a structural midpoint.

climax.description: Jack sits in a quiet hotel lobby with his new girlfriend Rebecca and friends including Lewis, spots Lady Fay Zhu and introduces her to the group, then talks with her one-on-one about her travels and lack of work while she mentions visiting the ocean to remember what's real.
climax.sceneHeading: 218 INT. HOTEL LOBBY - SANTA BARBARA - NIGHT 218

validation.ruling: Scene #142 (the Blockhouse descent) is horrific setup and escalating danger, but not the climactic confrontation itself. Scene #144 (hotel lobby with Jack, Rebecca, and Lady Fay Zhu) represents a quieter but more definitive climax: Jack's final moment of grace and connection before his decline, spotting Fay and having their last meaningful conversation about what's real—this is his ultimate confrontation with obsolescence and loss, demonstrating his arc's completion through dignified acceptance rather than violent action.

scenes.heading: 1 EXT. RURAL ROAD - DAY 1
scenes.summary: Manny arrives on a rural road with an elephant instead of the expected horse, surprising the truck driver who must now transport it.

scenes.heading: 2 EXT. RURAL ROAD - AN HOUR LATER 2
scenes.summary: The truck carrying the elephant struggles up a hill, sliding backward until Manny's lead car pulls it forward with a rope.

scenes.heading: 3 EXT. RURAL ROAD - ANOTHER HOUR LATER 3
scenes.summary: A cop stops Manny for transporting an elephant without a permit, but Manny negotiates by mentioning Don Wallach and the impressive guest list.

scenes.heading: 4 EXT. DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - NIGHT 4
scenes.summary: Manny arrives at Don Wallach's mansion with the elephant, accompanied by the cop, hillbillies, and others he's picked up along the way.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny arrives at the party that will introduce him to the world of Hollywood excess and key characters

scenes.heading: 5 INT. BEDROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - NIGHT 5
scenes.summary: A young woman named Jane urinates on an obese man's chest while he giggles, then snorts cocaine, causing her stream to hit his face.

scenes.heading: 6 INT. HALLWAY - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 6
scenes.summary: Orville Pickwick collides with Lady Fay Zhu in the hallway and she slaps him; Manny exits another door and Orville sends him to get more cocaine.

scenes.heading: 7 INT. BALLROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 7
scenes.summary: The ballroom is in chaos with drunken guests, a jazz band led by trumpeter Sidney Palmer, and a brawl that Sidney calmly avoids while continuing to play.

scenes.heading: 8 EXT. DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 8
scenes.summary: Manny catches a chicken outside, then meets Nellie LaRoy who crashes her car into a statue and demands entry to the party despite not being invited.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny meets Nellie LaRoy, a key character who will become a major star

scenes.heading: 10 INT. BALLROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 10
scenes.summary: Sidney and the bandleader discuss continuing to play while an elephant will stomp around the ballroom, with Sidney dismissing the saxophonist's safety concerns.

scenes.heading: 11 INT. BEDROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 11
scenes.summary: Sidney and the bandleader discuss continuing to play while an elephant will stomp around the ballroom, with Sidney dismissing the saxophonist's safety concerns.

scenes.heading: 12 INT. BACK ROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 12
scenes.summary: Jane is found unconscious with blood streaming from her nose while Orville weeps; Levine and Jimmy look for Manny to handle the situation.

scenes.heading: 13 EXT. DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - NIGHT 13
scenes.summary: Manny brings Nellie to a back room filled with drugs and she immediately snorts cocaine.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. BALLROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 14
scenes.summary: Jack Conrad arrives at the party with his wife Ina, exuding star power and cool detachment.

scenes.heading: 15 EXT. DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER 15
scenes.summary: Jack enters the ballroom and the entire party stops to focus on him as he navigates through adoring guests with practiced ease.

scenes.heading: 16 INT. BACK ROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 16
scenes.summary: Jack talks George Munn out of killing himself over a woman he met a week ago by mentioning another girl is asking after him.

scenes.heading: 17 INT. BALLROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 17
scenes.summary: Nellie snorts cocaine and talks with Manny, who admits he's never been on a movie set and wants to be part of something bigger.

scenes.heading: 19 INT. BEDROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER 19
scenes.summary: Lady Fay Zhu performs a sexually suggestive song about a cat to an erupting crowd, then kisses a woman in the audience.

scenes.heading: 20 INT. BALLROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER 20
scenes.summary: The elephant enters the ballroom as planned, drawing all attention while Levine and Jimmy carry the unconscious Jane down the staircase unnoticed.

scenes.heading: 21 INT. BALLROOM - DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - A FEW HOURS LATER 21
scenes.summary: Manny, Levine, and Jimmy huddle around the unconscious Jane; Manny proposes using the elephant as a distraction to smuggle her out without being noticed.

scenes.heading: 22 EXT. DON WALLACH’S HOUSE - DAWN 22
scenes.summary: The elephant enters the ballroom as planned, drawing all attention while Levine and Jimmy carry the unconscious Jane down the staircase unnoticed.

scenes.heading: 23 OMIT 23
scenes.summary: The party winds down with guests dancing, drinking, and using heroin; Jack drunkenly attempts to reach the unmanned bar.

scenes.heading: 24 EXT. ROAD - EARLY MORNING 24
scenes.summary: At dawn, Nellie drunkenly shouts at people from her past in Lafayette while Manny watches and realizes he's in love with her; she drives away.

scenes.heading: 25 EXT. JACK CONRAD’S HOUSE - EARLY MORNING 25
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 27 INT. BEDROOM - NELLIE’S APARTMENT - DAY 27
scenes.summary: Manny drives with a passed-out Jack in the passenger seat.

scenes.heading: 28 OMITTED 28
scenes.summary: Manny carries Jack into his mansion, but Jack suddenly breaks free and runs inside, playing loud opera and stripping while singing and talking about his wife's family.

scenes.heading: 30 INT. SAM WONG LAUNDRY - CHINATOWN - CONTINUOUS 30
scenes.summary: At the laundry, a customer argues with Fay's father Sam Wong Zhu about a ruined shirt until he recognizes Lady Fay and becomes starstruck.

scenes.heading: 31 INT. TENEMENT APARTMENT - BOYLE HEIGHTS - MORNING 31
scenes.summary: Nellie wakes after one hour of sleep in her cramped apartment, passes her drunk father Robert, and leaves.

scenes.heading: 32 OMIT 32
scenes.summary: Lady Fay Zhu writes in a notebook at her modest home in Chinatown, then joins her parents to help at their laundry.

scenes.heading: 33 INT. JACK CONRAD’S HOUSE - MORNING 33
scenes.summary: Jack wakes Manny with a hard shoulder slap and announces it's time to make a movie.

scenes.heading: 34 EXT. KINOSCOPE PICTURES LOT - DAY 34
scenes.summary: At the laundry, a customer argues with Fay's father Sam Wong Zhu about a ruined shirt until he recognizes Lady Fay and becomes starstruck.

scenes.heading: 35 OMITTED 35
scenes.summary: Sidney returns to his decrepit tenement apartment, leaves his cash on a table, and goes to sleep using chairs as a bed.

scenes.heading: 36 OMITTED 36
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 37 EXT. BATTLEFIELD - OUTSKIRTS OF LOS ANGELES - DAY 37
scenes.summary: Jack wakes Manny with a hard shoulder slap and announces it's time to make a movie.

scenes.heading: 38 EXT. SHOOTING STAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 38
scenes.summary: Nellie arrives at Kinoscope Pictures lot at 8 AM, walking past crowds of wannabes and protestors to multiple film sets shooting simultaneously.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nellie and Manny enter the world of film production, beginning their professional journeys

scenes.heading: 39 EXT. BATTLEFIELD - DAY 39
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 42 INT. JACK CONRAD’S TENT - CONTINUOUS 42
scenes.summary: Director Otto Von Strassberger yells in frustration on set.

scenes.heading: 43 EXT. BATTLEFIELD - CONTINUOUS 43
scenes.summary: Jack drives Manny recklessly to a battlefield set where director Otto Von Strassberger is shooting; producer George Munn greets them.

scenes.heading: 44 EXT. SHOOTING STAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 44
scenes.summary: Director Ruth Adler sees Nellie's face caked in excessive greasepaint and screams, ordering her makeup fixed and the schedule flipped.

scenes.heading: 45 EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - DAY 45
scenes.summary: The battle scene shoots with extras going wild, fighting violently with medieval weapons while an orchestra plays; injuries pile up in the first-aid tent.

scenes.heading: 57 INT. CAMERA RENTAL HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER 57
scenes.summary: Manny rushes to a camera rental house asking for a Bell & Howell camera but doesn't know which model, causing the employees to laugh at him.

scenes.heading: 58 INT. CAMERA RENTAL HOUSE - LATE AFTERNOON 58
scenes.summary: One hour later, Manny is still waiting at the camera rental house.

scenes.heading: 59 EXT. BATTLEFIELD - LATE AFTERNOON 59
scenes.summary: Jack pitches ideas in his tent while being fitted for costume as the battle rages outside; Irving Thalberg warns him against using prosthetics.

scenes.heading: 60 EXT. SHOOTING STAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - LATE AFTERNOON 60
scenes.summary: All five cameras are destroyed by horses; the crew realizes they need a replacement camera but their driver Joey is injured.

scenes.heading: 61 INT. CAMERA RENTAL HOUSE - LATE AFTERNOON 61
scenes.summary: Ruth Adler rushes Nellie through instructions for her scene: flirt with men, dance, get on a table, and don't look at the camera.

scenes.heading: 62 OMIT 62
scenes.summary: Manny floors a camera car toward town, honking at other drivers; Jack works with a writer while drinking; Nellie begins her scene confidently.

scenes.heading: 63 EXT. SHOOTING STAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - LATE AFTERNOON 63
scenes.summary: At 5:33 PM, Nellie performs her scene and bursts into genuine tears when she sees Constance Moore, despite having no glycerine.

scenes.heading: 64 EXT. BATTLEFIELD - LATE AFTERNOON 64
scenes.summary: At 5:48 PM, Manny arrives with the camera in an ambulance; Nellie cries on cue in take after take; Jack shoots his scene in the last five minutes of sunlight.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny saves the production by delivering the camera in time; both Nellie and Jack successfully complete their crucial scenes

scenes.heading: 83 INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT 83
scenes.summary: At Jack's wrap party, he introduces Manny to a model named Delphine, who is starstruck by Jack's attention.

scenes.heading: 84 INT. BACK ROOM OF THE PARTY - AN HOUR LATER 84
scenes.summary: Manny and Delphine have sex in a back room.

scenes.heading: 85 INT. SMALLER RESTAURANT - NIGHT 85
scenes.summary: At Nellie's lower-rent wrap party, Don Wallach reluctantly points out Nellie to a reporter as she humps an ice sculpture.

scenes.heading: 86 OMIT 86
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 87 OMIT 87
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 88 OMIT 88
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 89 INT. CUTTING ROOM - DAY 89
scenes.summary: Jack reviews battlefield footage with an editor and Manny, then asks Manny to send two dozen roses daily to Olga Putti despite her not speaking English.

scenes.heading: 90 INT. SMALLER CUTTING ROOM - DAY 90
scenes.summary: Lady Fay Zhu directs an editor on title cards for Nellie's footage and wonders aloud if Nellie is bisexual.

scenes.heading: 92 OMIT 92
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 93 OMIT 93
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 94 EXT. MOVIE THEATER - NIGHT 94
scenes.summary: Nellie and her father Robert are denied entry to a premiere until a young fan recognizes Nellie and asks for her autograph.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. MOVIE THEATER - NIGHT 95
scenes.summary: The audience watches Nellie's performance onscreen with growing enthusiasm, erupting into cheers as Nellie watches and devours their applause.

scenes.heading: 96 EXT. MOVIE THEATER - NIGHT 96
scenes.summary: Nellie exits to new fans while Robert holds court with reporters, claiming credit for her success and correcting her stage name.

scenes.heading: 99 INT. SHOOTING STAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 99
scenes.summary: Constance Moore accuses Nellie of stealing scenes, icing her nipples, and planting a giant dildo in her dressing room during a shoot.

scenes.heading: 100 INT. PHONE BOOTH - THAT EVENING 100
scenes.summary: Nellie calls Dr. Lubin from a phone booth to schedule the operation they discussed for that night.

scenes.heading: 101 EXT. SHOOTING STAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - THE NEXT MORNING 101
scenes.summary: Nellie arrives on set with her face completely bandaged, causing Constance's production to shut down and Nellie's new picture to begin.

scenes.heading: 103 EXT. NEW MOVIE SET - DAY 103
scenes.summary: Nellie suggests trying a close-up after shooting a medium shot, and no one on the crew dares refuse her.

scenes.heading: 104 INT. ELINOR ST. JOHN’S OFFICE - DAY 104
scenes.summary: Elinor dictates a column praising Nellie's vitality, then visits Nellie's chaotic new home where Nellie shows off her eclectic possessions and expensive perfume.

scenes.heading: 107 INT. PUBLIC BATHROOM - NIGHT 107
scenes.summary: A fat man masturbates to Nellie's photos in a bathroom stall while Jack talks with a studio exec who mentions Jolson's sound picture, sparking Jack's sudden interest.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack learns about sound pictures for the first time, setting in motion the central conflict of the film's second half.

scenes.heading: 108 INT. JACK CONRAD’S HOUSE - DAY 108
scenes.summary: Jack passionately pitches sound as the future of cinema to George and Manny while Olga screams and throws dishes in the kitchen, and Manny translates her Hungarian curses.

scenes.heading: 109 OMITTED 109
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 110 INT. ELEVATOR - OFFICE BUILDING - NEW YORK - DAY 110
scenes.summary: Manny rides an elevator in a New York office building, nervous as he ascends to the 45th floor.

scenes.heading: 111 INT. ELEVATOR LOBBY - OFFICE BUILDING - NEW YORK - DAY 111
scenes.summary: A technician greets Manny enthusiastically, mistaking his name, and leads him past a door marked Western Electric while hyping the top-secret sound technology.

scenes.heading: 112 INT. WESTERN ELECTRIC OFFICES - NEW YORK - DAY 112
scenes.summary: Manny watches a sound short film where a man sings with a quacking duck, but the sync goes off and technicians struggle to explain the projection process.

scenes.heading: 113 INT. WARNER THEATRE - NEW YORK - DAY 113
scenes.summary: Manny observes ear-piercing distortion during a sound check at the Warner Theatre and learns the premiere is still scheduled for that night.

scenes.heading: 114 EXT. WARNER THEATRE - NEW YORK - LATE AFTERNOON 114
scenes.summary: Manny calls Jack in the pouring rain to report that sound doesn't work and isn't ready, then spots Nellie surrounded by crazed fans.

scenes.heading: 115 INT. NELLIE’S CAR / EXT. SANATORIUM - LATE AFTERNOON 115
scenes.summary: Nellie and Manny reunite in her car, and she explains she's in New York escaping LA drama and visiting her mother at a sanatorium.

scenes.heading: 116 INT. THACKERAY SANATORIUM - DUSK 116
scenes.summary: Nellie visits her mother at the sanatorium, but the frail woman doesn't recognize her and says nothing before being led away by a nurse.

scenes.heading: 118 INT. WARNER THEATRE - NIGHT 118
scenes.summary: Manny enters the theater mid-screening, distracted by thoughts of Nellie, then gradually notices the audience erupting in applause around him.

scenes.heading: 119 EXT. WARNER THEATRE - NIGHT 119
scenes.summary: Manny rushes to a payphone beneath a billboard for The Jazz Singer and calls Jack again.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny witnesses the successful premiere of The Jazz Singer, confirming sound cinema's viability and the industry's imminent transformation.

scenes.heading: 120 INT. SOUND BOOTH - DAY 120
scenes.summary: An actress trembles as she reads Little Bo Peep into a large microphone in a sound booth in May 1928.

scenes.heading: 121 EXT. FIELD - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 121
scenes.summary: Don Wallach addresses contract players about sound technology while actors continue reading Little Bo Peep in the sound booth, and he announces mandatory voice tests.

scenes.heading: 122 OMIT 122
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 123 EXT. BACKYARD/POOL - JACK CONRAD’S HOUSE - NIGHT 123
scenes.summary: Jack introduces Manny to his new fiancée Estelle at a pool party, where Jack drunkenly argues that movies are as profound as theater and sound could revolutionize the medium.

scenes.heading: 125 OMIT 125
scenes.summary: Fay glides through the party eyeing guests while Nellie watches her father Robert hit on a young girl, then Fay and Nellie lock eyes.

scenes.heading: 127 OMIT 127
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 128 OMIT 128
scenes.summary: Nellie locks herself in a room, tries to breathe through what seems like fear, then bursts into laughter while Fay processes outside and Thalberg drunkenly offers Sidney a job.

scenes.heading: 133 EXT. DESERT - NIGHT 133
scenes.summary: A caravan of cars drives through the desert at night.

scenes.heading: 134 INT. FIRST CAR - DESERT - CONTINUOUS 134
scenes.summary: Nellie discusses male anatomy with a mortified Estelle while Jack drives recklessly at 100 mph hunting for rattlesnakes with Manny and George.

scenes.heading: 135 INT. SECOND CAR - DESERT - CONTINUOUS 135
scenes.summary: Elinor sits squeezed between footballers while Robert drunkenly lectures an uneasy Liz about rattlesnake combat techniques.

scenes.heading: 136 EXT. DESERT - MOMENTS LATER 136
scenes.summary: Nellie finds a rattlesnake and the group gathers as Robert approaches it to demonstrate combat while Nellie watches hungrily, then Robert is bitten and Nellie grabs the snake and bites its head off.

scenes.heading: 137 OMIT 137
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 138 OMIT 138
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 139 INT. CAR - OUTSKIRTS OF LOS ANGELES - DAY 139
scenes.summary: Fay drives a dazed, red-eyed Nellie with a bandaged neck back toward Los Angeles and confirms her call time.

scenes.heading: 140 EXT. KINOSCOPE PICTURES LOT - DAY 140
scenes.summary: Fay and Nellie arrive at the eerily quiet lot and learn from a shocked stagehand that the studio is closed until they build the soundstages.
scenes.turningPointNote: The studio's closure to build soundstages marks the industry's full commitment to sound and the end of the silent era's working methods.

scenes.heading: 141 INT. SOUNDSTAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 141
scenes.summary: Nellie struggles through multiple takes on a silent soundstage in oppressive heat as the director and crew demand she hit precise marks for the microphone while staying natural.

scenes.heading: 144 INT. SOUNDSTAGE / CUTTING ROOM - MGM STUDIOS - DAY 144
scenes.summary: Manny finds Jack in a cutting room watching The Passion of Joan of Arc and tries to get him to the soundstage, but Jack is absorbed by the film's innovative use of close-ups.

scenes.heading: 145 INT. SOUNDSTAGE - MGM STUDIOS - MOMENTS LATER 145
scenes.summary: Jack watches in horror as actors sing and twirl umbrellas under a rain machine in front of a Noah's Ark backdrop, questioning the absurdity before reluctantly joining them.

scenes.heading: 146 INT. NEW MOVIE SET - MGM SOUNDSTAGE - DAY 146
scenes.summary: Sidney performs a fiery jazz solo on camera while Manny watches with Thalberg, who smiles in approval and shakes Manny's hand after the take.

scenes.heading: 147 EXT. MGM SOUNDSTAGE - DAY 147
scenes.summary: Manny exits with new swagger when Levine appears and asks if the Sidney Palmer shorts were Manny's idea, offering to introduce him to Thalberg.

scenes.heading: 148 OMIT 148
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 149 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 149
scenes.summary: Manny pitches Kinoscope's top brass on transforming Nellie into a sophisticated lady through voice coaching, image change, and Elinor St. John's endorsement.

scenes.heading: 150 INT. HALLWAY - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - CONTINUOUS 150
scenes.summary: Manny meets Nellie in the hallway as Wallach introduces them, and Nellie recognizes Manny as a misfit like herself among the old white men in suits.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny's formal introduction to Nellie as a studio executive marks his transformation from outsider to insider and sets up his role in attempting to save her career.

scenes.heading: 151 INT. 18TH-CENTURY SET - SOUNDSTAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 151
scenes.summary: Manny directs Nellie on an 18th-century soundstage set while workers stencil his name on the sound booth door and he receives a phone call.

scenes.heading: 152 INT. NELLIE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT 152
scenes.summary: Nellie practices her mid-Atlantic accent with Lady Fay Zhu's help while a Variety magazine on the floor shows Sidney Palmer's new Kinoscope deal.

scenes.heading: 153 INT. SIDNEY’S NEW HOUSE - CENTRAL ALAMEDA - DAY 153
scenes.summary: Sidney arrives at a stunning new house gifted to him by the studio, along with keys to a gleaming Rolls Royce Phantom.

scenes.heading: 154 INT. MANNY’S OFFICE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 154
scenes.summary: Manny works in his new office when a Kinoscope executive shows him a tabloid with a headline about Nellie and Fay, asking what to do about Fay Zhu.

scenes.heading: 155 INT. SIDNEY’S NEW HOUSE - CENTRAL ALAMEDA - DAY 155
scenes.summary: Sidney stands alone in his new empty house, processing what it feels like to be there.

scenes.heading: 156 INT. JACK CONRAD’S HOUSE - DAY 156
scenes.summary: Jack rehearses lines from a script with a cocktail in hand while Estelle reads nearby on a couch.

scenes.heading: 157 INT. IRVING THALBERG’S OFFICE - MGM STUDIOS - DAY 157
scenes.summary: Irving Thalberg dictates voice notes about various actors to a stenographer at his MGM desk.

scenes.heading: 158 INT. SOUNDSTAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 158
scenes.summary: Manny confronts Lady Fay backstage about the tabloid, telling her the relationship with Nellie is unacceptable due to new moral sensibilities, while Jack rehearses lines, Thalberg dictates notes about Marion Davies, and Jack kisses his costar onscreen.

scenes.heading: 167 INT. SOUNDSTAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 167
scenes.summary: Lady Fay walks through the soundstage to the exit after being fired, holding her pain within, and the door closes with a 'Silence Please' sign visible.

scenes.heading: 168 INT. CUTTING ROOM - MGM STUDIOS - DAY 168
scenes.summary: Jack watches his latest film footage in an MGM cutting room, gives editing notes, discusses the work with the director, then leaves to repair his marriage before heading to a Hearst party where guests surround Sidney asking about his musical influences.

scenes.heading: 173 OMIT 173
scenes.summary: Guests question Sidney about his influences while Nellie nervously eats and excuses herself to the powder room, Manny follows her but misses Jack's arrival with Estelle, and Jack enthusiastically greets guests about his European trip.

scenes.heading: 184 EXT. GAZEBO - JACK’S HOUSE - DAY 184
scenes.summary: Jack gives an interview in his gazebo, discussing his recent failures, his commitment to learning sound filmmaking, and his decision to quit drinking.

scenes.heading: 185 INT. IRVING THALBERG’S OFFICE - MGM STUDIOS - DAY 185
scenes.summary: Jack reads Elinor St. John's article titled 'Is Jack Conrad Through?' while Thalberg angrily calls her names and hands Jack a new script for 'A Stranger Calls.'

scenes.heading: 186 INT. SIDNEY’S DRESSING ROOM - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 186
scenes.summary: Sidney sits alone in his dressing room staring into space with uncertainty in his eyes, surrounded by trumpet mutes and sheet music, until a PA knocks to call him to set.

scenes.heading: 187 EXT. KINOSCOPE PICTURES / INT. NELLIE’S DRESSING ROOM - CONTINUOUS 187
scenes.summary: Manny enters Nellie's dressing room where she is high, counting cash, and planning to go to Cal-Neva to gamble, and they argue violently about her behavior and career.

scenes.heading: 188 INT. JACK CONRAD’S HOUSE - DAY 188
scenes.summary: Jack sits in his kitchen at 10:00 AM with a martini, calling Irving Thalberg's office and being told to try back at one o'clock.

scenes.heading: 189 INT. SOUNDSTAGES - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 189
scenes.summary: Manny does cocaine while hurrying across a bank vault set, reassuring Wallach about Nellie, then testing a prop gun that still sounds wrong.

scenes.heading: 190 INT. JACK CONRAD’S HOUSE - DAY 190
scenes.summary: Jack calls Thalberg's office again at 1:00 PM and is told to try back at four, while looking at a Variety headline about his third straight flop.

scenes.heading: 191 INT. SOUNDSTAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 191
scenes.summary: Manny hands Sidney burnt cork to darken his face for lighting purposes, claiming it's just for one wide shot, and Sidney reluctantly agrees after Manny promises future creative control.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sidney is asked to perform in blackface, a degrading compromise that marks his complicity in the industry's racism and foreshadows his breaking point.

scenes.heading: 192 INT. JACK CONRAD’S HOUSE - DAY 192
scenes.summary: Jack calls Thalberg's office at 4:00 PM, is told he's gone for the day, and decides to go see for himself.

scenes.heading: 193 EXT. ROAD - DAY 193
scenes.summary: Jack drives at 100 miles per hour.

scenes.heading: 194 INT. SOUNDSTAGE - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 194
scenes.summary: Sidney plays his horn with his face coated in cork under the lights, then lowers it and pauses as if weighing whether to continue before raising it back to his lips and resuming.

scenes.heading: 195 EXT. NELLIE’S DRESSING ROOM - KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 195
scenes.summary: Manny frantically asks where Nellie went as Sidney walks to his parked Rolls Royce, places the keys on the windshield, and keeps walking away.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sidney abandons his studio car and walks away from his career, rejecting the compromises he's been forced to make.

scenes.heading: 196 OMIT 196
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 197 OMIT 197
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 198 INT. IRVING THALBERG’S OFFICE - MGM STUDIOS - DAY 198
scenes.summary: Jack bursts into Thalberg's empty office, sees poster mockups including one for 'Red Dust' starring Clark Gable, and examines scripts on the desk.

scenes.heading: 199 EXT. ROAD - DAY 199
scenes.summary: Jack drives at 150 miles per hour with an eerie calm in his eyes.

scenes.heading: 200 OMIT 200
scenes.summary: A PA tells Manny that Nellie may have gone to Cal-Neva, Manny explodes in rage and throws the phone as Wallach arrives, while Sidney hands his employee pass to a guard and walks off the Kinoscope lot.

scenes.heading: 202 OMIT 202
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 203 OMIT 203
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 204 OMIT 204
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 205 INT. ELINOR ST. JOHN’S OFFICE - EARLY EVENING 205
scenes.summary: Jack enters Elinor St. John's office at 5:00 PM and confronts her about her article, accusing her of something beyond their usual transactional relationship, and she asks what he really wants to know.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack confronts Elinor about her devastating article, seeking answers about why the industry has turned on him — a moment of reckoning with his obsolescence.

scenes.heading: 207 INT. STUDY - JACK CONRAD’S HOUSE - NIGHT 207
scenes.summary: Jack sits alone in his study in a bathrobe listening to Verdi, gazing at a photo of himself with George Munn, watching a lizard in the garden until it scurries away, then answers a call from Irving Thalberg who apologizes for not talking and offers him a supporting role.

scenes.heading: 208 OMIT 208
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 209 INT. LIVING ROOM - MANNY’S HOUSE - DAY 209
scenes.summary: Nellie, high on Manny's cocaine, dances manically in his living room proposing stage work while Manny tries to stop her and hide the drugs, and they argue violently about her behavior and his attempts to help her career.

scenes.heading: 210 EXT. BEACH MOVIE SET - SANTA BARBARA - DAY 210
scenes.summary: Jack sits alone on a secluded beach at 10:00 AM, gets his makeup done by Harold for their 82nd movie together, waits through a thirty-minute delay, then arrives on set for a quick rehearsal of a drowning scene.

scenes.heading: 211 EXT. THE GARDEN OF ALLAH - LAUREL CANYON - NIGHT 211
scenes.summary: Manny and the Count arrive nervously at the Garden of Allah hotel to meet Wilson, who works for McKay, planning to hand over a bag to eliminate a threat.

scenes.heading: 212 OMIT 212
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 213 EXT. COURTYARD - THE GARDEN OF ALLAH - MOMENTS LATER 213
scenes.summary: The Count and Manny meet Wilson in a courtyard and hand him a bag full of money, Wilson checks it and returns saying McKay wants to buy them a drink, and despite the Count's hesitation Manny agrees to follow Wilson to meet McKay.

scenes.heading: 214 INT. WILSON’S CAR - NIGHT 214
scenes.summary: Manny and the Count sit in Wilson's car as McKay discusses movie ideas while the car winds up into dark forest, and Manny realizes with terror that the bag on McKay's lap is filled with prop money.

scenes.heading: 215 EXT. THE BLOCKHOUSE - OUTSKIRTS OF LOS ANGELES - NIGHT 215
scenes.summary: Wilson's car pulls up to the Blockhouse, a hole in a hill in the middle of nowhere, and McKay invites them inside for a nightcap despite Manny and the Count's visible reluctance.

scenes.heading: 216 INT. THE BLOCKHOUSE - OUTSKIRTS OF LOS ANGELES - CONTINUOUS 216
scenes.summary: Manny and the Count follow McKay and Wilson through the Blockhouse, descending past a cage where women violently fight, down medieval stairwells past torture instruments and caged naked people, to a basement where a man performs with a rat, then McKay opens the money bag revealing it's fake and pulls a gun as Manny and the Count flee up the stairs while gunshots ring out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny's attempt to pay off Nellie's debt with prop money is discovered in a nightmarish underground den, forcing him to flee for his life — the consequences of Hollywood's corruption made visceral and inescapable.

scenes.heading: 217 EXT. THE BLOCKHOUSE - OUTSKIRTS OF LOS ANGELES - CONTINUOUS 217
scenes.summary: Manny and the Count rush to Wilson's car, dive in, start it as gunshots shatter the back windshield, and peel off into the night screaming.

scenes.heading: 218 INT. HOTEL LOBBY - SANTA BARBARA - NIGHT 218
scenes.summary: Jack sits in a quiet hotel lobby with his new girlfriend Rebecca and friends including Lewis, spots Lady Fay Zhu and introduces her to the group, then talks with her one-on-one about her travels and lack of work while she mentions visiting the ocean to remember what's real.

scenes.heading: 219 INT. MANNY’S HOUSE - MORNING 219
scenes.summary: Manny bursts into his house in the morning, wakes the passed-out and high Nellie, frantically gathers cash and her passport, and tells her they're going to Mexico while she resists.

scenes.heading: 220 EXT. MANNY’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 220
scenes.summary: Manny gets Nellie into the car where the Count is waiting, refuses to give her heroin, and tears out of the driveway.

scenes.heading: 221 OMIT 221
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 222 OMIT 222
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 223 OMIT 223
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 224 EXT. THE COUNT’S APARTMENT BUILDING - LATE AFTERNOON 224
scenes.summary: The car screeches to a stop at the Count's apartment building where he rushes out to pack while Manny and Nellie drive off to get gas, but they pass closed stations as Nellie does more cocaine and playfully smacks Manny with a pillow while he frantically searches for fuel and warns her they'll be killed if caught.

scenes.heading: 227 EXT. THE COUNT’S APARTMENT BUILDING - NIGHT 227
scenes.summary: Manny and Nellie return to the Count's building at night, exchange 'I love you's and kiss, then Manny runs inside while Nellie waits in the car before exiting with only her handbag, leaving the keys in the ignition.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nellie abandons Manny by leaving the car with keys inside while he's retrieving the Count, marking her final betrayal and disappearance from his life.

scenes.heading: 228 INT. THE COUNT’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS 228
scenes.summary: Manny enters the Count's apartment where the Count is filling a water jug and introduces his roommate Kyle; Manny notices headshots and discarded movie posters in the corner.

scenes.heading: 229 EXT. THE COUNT’S APARTMENT BUILDING - CONTINUOUS 229
scenes.summary: Manny runs to his car to find it empty with Nellie gone, calls her name, but this time doesn't chase after her and instead speeds away; a trumpet sound is heard in the silence.

scenes.heading: 230 INT. NIGHT CLUB - CENTRAL AVENUE - NIGHT 230
scenes.summary: Inside an understated bar and club on Central Avenue with an all-black crowd, a conductor introduces Sidney Palmer as a new addition to the Hart Café Band on trumpet.

scenes.heading: 231 INT. LADY FAY ZHU’S BUNGALOW - DAY 231
scenes.summary: Lady Fay packs her belongings including a notepad and childhood photo, then kisses her parents goodbye at Sam Wong Laundry and signs a final headshot for a customer before departing with tickets.

scenes.heading: 233 OMIT 233
scenes.summary: Elinor St. John's voiceover eulogizes Jack Conrad over a Photoplay magazine cover showing him in his prime with the word 'STAR' visible in the headline.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack Conrad's death is revealed through his obituary and magazine cover, marking the end of the silent film era's greatest star.

scenes.heading: 234 EXT. CEMETERY - DAY 234
scenes.summary: At Jack's burial ceremony, mourners including Estelle, Ina, Olga, Don Wallach, Thalberg, and Elinor gather; Elinor observes the young actor from Jack's last movie whispering to Thalberg and makes a note.

scenes.heading: 235 EXT. LOS ANGELES (STOCK FOOTAGE) 235
scenes.summary: Stock footage montage shows old Los Angeles transforming: Chinatown around Sam Wong Laundry demolished, Mexican migrants warded off streets, Union Station construction beginning, and new buildings rising as the old era is bulldozed away.

scenes.heading: 236 INT. NIGHT CLUB - CENTRAL AVENUE - NIGHT 236
scenes.summary: Sidney plays a dazzling trumpet solo cadenza ending on a triumphant high C as the band rejoins for the fermata.

scenes.heading: 237 OMIT 237
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 238 OMIT 238
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 239 EXT. LOS ANGELES - DAY 239
scenes.summary: In 1952, Manny in his 40s arrives by bus with his wife Silvia and seven-year-old daughter at the old Kinoscope Pictures gate, tells the new guard he used to work there, and asks what they're making now.
scenes.turningPointNote: Twenty years later, Manny returns to the studio where his journey began, now as a family man visiting from the outside, showing how far he's traveled from that world.

scenes.heading: 240 EXT. KINOSCOPE PICTURES - DAY 240
scenes.summary: An hour later, Manny slowly walks the perimeter of the Kinoscope Pictures studio lot.

scenes.heading: 241 OMITTED 241
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 242 EXT. CITY STREET - DAY 242
scenes.summary: Late afternoon, Manny passes an appliance store, pin-up shop with Marilyn Monroe images, and a movie theater; he stops, looks at the Art Deco theater entrance with Technicolor posters, continues walking, then wavers and turns back toward it.

scenes.heading: 243 INT. MOVIE THEATER AUDITORIUM - CONTINUOUS 243
scenes.summary: Manny enters the darkened theater where a chorus sings on screen in Technicolor with Gene Kelly and Debbie Reynolds; he observes the mostly young, enjoying audience around him, then hears a character in the movie mention 'The Jazz Singer' and turns back to the screen.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny re-enters a movie theater after years away and hears 'The Jazz Singer' referenced on screen, the film that began the sound revolution that destroyed so many lives, bringing his journey full circle.
